mnemonics: CRUD on Lists: Create, Read, Update, Delete. The four basic operations you perform on any data collection, just like with lists.
keyPoints: Lists are mutable sequences that can be modified after creation.
Indexing (my_list[index]) retrieves a single element, while slicing (my_list[start:end]) retrieves a sub-list.
Methods like .append(), .extend(), .insert(), .remove(), and .pop() are essential for modifying lists.
commonMistakes: Using the wrong index and causing an IndexError (e.g., trying to access my_list[len(my_list)]).
Forgetting that methods like .append() modify the list in-place and return None, not the modified list.
Confusing .extend() (which adds elements from an iterable) with .append() (which adds its argument as a single element).
